name: American Trucking Associations v. City of Los Angeles
question:
Can a municipal government limit the activities of motor carriers when it acts as a market participant, as opposed to a market regulator?

Can a municipal government bar federally licensed motor carriers from access to a port?
description: A case in which the Court held that when a municipal government is acting as a contract-based participant in the market, as opposed to a market regulator, it could not regulate motor carrier activities.
